design_name: 90-degree pipe elbow with annular cross-section swept along a quarter-circle arc in the XZ plane. Outer diameter 20mm, inner diameter 16mm (2mm wall), bend centerline radius 40mm.
prompt: A 90-degree pipe elbow with the bend in the XZ plane. The pipe has a 20mm outer diameter and 16mm inner diameter (2mm wall thickness). The bend centerline follows a quarter-circle arc of 40mm radius on the XZ plane: starting at the origin heading in the +Z direction, curving through the first quadrant, and ending at (X=40, Z=40) heading in the +X direction. The pipe cross-section is centered on this arc. The part is centered at Y=0. Created by sweeping the annular pipe cross-section along the arc path defined by a radiusArc from (0,0) to (40,40) with radius 40 on the XZ workplane.
primitive_plan_summary: 90-degree pipe elbow with annular cross-section swept along a quarter-circle arc in the XZ plane. Outer diameter 20mm, inner diameter 16mm (2mm wall), bend centerline radius 40mm.

verifier_score.feedback: All constraints met. The pipe elbow is correctly constructed as a 90-degree bend in the XZ plane. Key verification: (1) Bounding box: X ranges from -10 to 40 (50mm span = 40mm bend radius + 10mm pipe outer radius), Z ranges from ~0 to 50 (40mm + 10mm), Y ranges from -10 to 10 (20mm outer diameter) — all consistent with specifications. (2) The part is centered at Y=0 as required. (3) Wall thickness: outer radius 10mm, inner radius 8mm gives 2mm wall thickness. (4) Volume ~7106 mm³ is physically plausible for a quarter-torus annular section (theoretical: π/2 * R * (r_outer² - r_inner²) = π/2 * 40 * (100-64) ≈ 2262 mm³... actually let me recalculate: V = π²/2 * R * (r_o² - r_i²) — no, the volume of a quarter torus shell is (π/2) * 2π * R * (r_o² - r_i²)/2... The exact formula is V = (π²/2) * R_bend * (r_outer² - r_inner²) = (π²/2) * 40 * (100 - 64) = (4.9348) * 40 * 36 ≈ 7106 mm³, which matches perfectly. (5) The rendered images clearly show a hollow pipe elbow with the correct 90-degree bend, visible hollow bore in the isometric and front views, and proper proportions. The part is valid, watertight, with no self-intersections.

(2) The part is centered at Y=0 as required. (3) Wall thickness: outer radius 10mm, inner radius 8mm gives 2mm wall thickness. (4) Volume ~7106 mm³ is physically plausible for a quarter-torus annular section (theoretical: π/2 * R * (r_outer² - r_inner²) = π/2 * 40 * (100-64) ≈ 2262 mm³... actually let me recalculate: V = π²/2 * R * (r_o² - r_i²) — no, the volume of a quarter torus shell is (π/2) * 2π * R * (r_o² - r_i²)/2... The exact formula is V = (π²/2) * R_bend * (r_outer² - r_inner²) = (π²/2) * 40 * (100 - 64) = (4.9348) * 40 * 36 ≈ 7106 mm³, which matches perfectly.
